title: San Diego 14-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: San Diego 14-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to San Diego, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including San Diego Zoo and San Diego Zoo Safari Park. Prices from $2444 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering San Diego, United States — Mild semi-arid Mediterranean climate, one of the most consistent in the US: warm, dry summers (highs 25-27°C with low humidity) and mild winters (highs 18-19°C), with minimal rainfall concentrated Dec-Mar. March to May and September to November for warm, dry, uncrowded weather (highs 21-24°C); San Diego's mild climate makes it a solid year-round destination, though June brings a brief "June gloom" morning marine layer. This itinerary blends San Diego Zoo, San Diego Zoo Safari Park, Balboa Park with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including San Diego Zoo, San Diego Zoo Safari Park, Balboa Park
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in San Diego
itinerary.description: Land at San Diego International Airport (SAN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to San Diego Zoo — World-renowned zoo in Balboa Park with pandas and a globally recognized conservation program. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: San Diego: San Diego Zoo Safari Park & USS Midway Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to San Diego Zoo Safari Park — Large safari-style wildlife park in Escondido, sister site to the main zoo. In the afternoon, continue to USS Midway Museum — Retired aircraft carrier turned floating naval aviation museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: San Diego: SeaWorld San Diego & Birch Aquarium at Scripps
itinerary.description: Morning visit to SeaWorld San Diego — Marine life theme park with shows and rides. In the afternoon, continue to Birch Aquarium at Scripps — Oceanographic aquarium affiliated with UC San Diego. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Tijuana, Mexico (border crossing)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Tijuana, Mexico (border crossing), a popular side trip from San Diego. Return to San Diego in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Tijuana, Mexico (border crossing)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Sunset Cliffs tide pools — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: San Diego: Cabrillo National Monument & Legoland California, Carlsbad
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Cabrillo National Monument — Historic lighthouse and tide pools at Point Loma, with whale-watching overlooks. In the afternoon, continue to Legoland California, Carlsbad — Family theme park roughly 35 minutes north of downtown. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Julian mountain town
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Julian mountain town, a popular side trip from San Diego. Return to San Diego in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Julian mountain town

itinerary.title: San Diego: Balboa Park & La Jolla Cove
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Balboa Park — Sprawling cultural park with Spanish Colonial architecture and gardens (individual museums ticketed). In the afternoon, continue to La Jolla Cove — Scenic cove famous for resident sea lions and seals. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Spanish Village Art Center, Balboa Park — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Spanish Village Art Center, Balboa Park

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Temecula wine country
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Temecula wine country, a popular side trip from San Diego. Return to San Diego in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Temecula wine country

itinerary.title: San Diego: Gaslamp Quarter & Sunset Cliffs Natural Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Gaslamp Quarter — Historic downtown entertainment district. In the afternoon, continue to Sunset Cliffs Natural Park — Dramatic coastal cliffs, especially photogenic at sunset. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: San Diego: Coronado Beach & Old Town San Diego State Historic Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Coronado Beach — Wide sandy beach facing the historic Hotel del Coronado. In the afternoon, continue to Old Town San Diego State Historic Park — Reconstructed original Spanish/Mexican-era settlement. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Old Town San Diego State Historic Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Mission Trails Regional Park — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve trails or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SAN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy a combined San Diego Zoo + Safari Park ticket if visiting both — it offers meaningful savings over separate admission.
Visit La Jolla Cove early morning for the best sea lion activity and easiest parking.
Balboa Park's museums offer a rotating free-admission Tuesday schedule by museum — check the current calendar before visiting.
Coronado is easily reached by a scenic ferry from downtown's Broadway Pier rather than driving over the bridge.
December-April is prime gray whale migration season for whale-watching boat tours departing from the harbor.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Light layers — mornings can be cool and foggy even on warm days ("June gloom")
Swimwear and reef-safe sunscreen for beach time
Comfortable walking shoes for Balboa Park and coastal trails
A passport if planning a same-day Tijuana day trip
thingsToDo: Old Town San Diego State Historic Park
Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve trails
Surfing lessons at Pacific Beach or La Jolla Shores
Kayaking the La Jolla sea caves
Hiking Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve
Whale watching boat tours (Dec-Apr gray whale migration)
thingsToAvoid: Getting too close to sea lions/seals at La Jolla Cove — they are wild and protected, with fines for harassment.
Driving into Mexico without proper documentation and Mexican auto insurance — walk across or use a trolley/pedestrian crossing instead.
Underestimating June/July marine layer ("June gloom") — mornings can stay grey and cool into early afternoon.
Swimming at Sunset Cliffs — the area is known for strong currents and is better suited to sightseeing than swimming.
Assuming public transit alone covers the whole region — La Jolla, Coronado and inland attractions are much easier with a car.
topRestaurants: Hodad's — American Burgers, Ocean Beach institution ($)
The Fish Market, San Diego — Seafood, waterfront ($$$)
Lucha Libre Taco Shop — Mexican Street Food ($)
George's at the Cove, La Jolla — Californian, ocean-view fine dining ($$$$)
nightlife: Gaslamp Quarter bar and club strip
instagramSpots: Sunset Cliffs Natural Park at golden hour
Coronado Beach with Hotel del Coronado
hiddenGems: Spanish Village Art Center, Balboa Park
adventureActivities: Surfing lessons at Pacific Beach or La Jolla Shores
Kayaking the La Jolla sea caves
Hiking Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve
Whale watching boat tours (Dec-Apr gray whale migration)
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the historic Hotel del Coronado
Private sunset sailing charter on San Diego Bay
Fine dining at George's at the Cove overlooking La Jolla
Private guided San Diego Zoo VIP experience
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset at Sunset Cliffs Natural Park
Private sailing trip around San Diego Bay
Couple's spa day at a La Jolla resort
budgetExperiences: Free entry to Balboa Park grounds and gardens
Free sea lion viewing at La Jolla Cove
Coronado Beach and Sunset Cliffs sunset watching
Old Town San Diego State Historic Park self-guided walk

festivalCalendar.name: San Diego Bay Wine & Food Festival

localEtiquette: Beach towns like La Jolla and Pacific Beach have a relaxed, casual dress culture even at nicer restaurants.
Respect posted seal/sea lion viewing distances at La Jolla Cove — approaching too closely is both risky and can disturb protected wildlife.
San Diego sits right at the US-Mexico border — carry a passport if planning a same-day Tijuana crossing.
localTransportGuide: San Diego is more spread out than downtown-centric cities, so renting a car is common for reaching La Jolla, Coronado and Torrey Pines; the compact San Diego Trolley and MTS buses cover downtown, the Gaslamp Quarter and the airport reasonably well. Coronado is reachable by the iconic Coronado Bridge or a scenic ferry from downtown.
bestTimeToVisit: March to May and September to November for warm, dry, uncrowded weather (highs 21-24°C); San Diego's mild climate makes it a solid year-round destination, though June brings a brief "June gloom" morning marine layer.
weather: Mild semi-arid Mediterranean climate, one of the most consistent in the US: warm, dry summers (highs 25-27°C with low humidity) and mild winters (highs 18-19°C), with minimal rainfall concentrated Dec-Mar.
visaInformation: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview.
averageFlightTime: ~15.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit San Diego?
faqs.answer: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit San Diego?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to November for warm, dry, uncrowded weather (highs 21-24°C); San Diego's mild climate makes it a solid year-round destination, though June brings a brief "June gloom" morning marine layer. Mild semi-arid Mediterranean climate, one of the most consistent in the US: warm, dry summers (highs 25-27°C with low humidity) and mild winters (highs 18-19°C), with minimal rainfall concentrated Dec-Mar.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is San Diego safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: San Diego is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to San Diego?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include San Diego Zoo.

reviews.author: Arjun Kim
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Coronado Beach in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Elena Sharma
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Balboa Park and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ethan Rossi
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Legoland California, Carlsbad and Birch Aquarium at Scripps. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Wei Wang
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Balboa Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sofia Khan
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Birch Aquarium at Scripps in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Grace Larsen
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond San Diego Zoo Safari Park and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mohammed Müller
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: SeaWorld San Diego alone was worth the trip, but combined with Birch Aquarium at Scripps this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Anna van Dijk
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: San Diego is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Balboa Park. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Lucas Botha
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve trails and Balboa Park. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Liam van Dijk
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Our trip to San Diego was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Cabrillo National Monument was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
